rationale: The citing paper explicitly positions itself as extending prior mindset research to creative performance and task choice, and the cited paper is a mindset study that also links induced mind-sets to creativity-related processing and downstream consumer evaluations. Given this clear conceptual overlap (mind-set carryover and creativity mechanism), this looks plausibly theory/mechanism-relevant rather than an obvious incidental mention, even though the raw evidence only provides a DOI-level match.
